binaries.title: mrxsmb.sys (SMB Redirector) - No security-relevant change (reparse-tag classification tweak and SMB-over-QUIC teardown feature-staging)

changed_functions.name: FsRtlValidateReparsePointBufferEx
key_changes: Removed a single tag-acceptance predicate term: (ReparseTag & 0xC0000000) != 0x40000000
Symbolic-link (0xA000000C) and mount-point (0xA0000003) offset/length validation (RtlULongAdd accumulation and bounds comparisons) is byte-identical in both builds
Only other deltas are WPP tracing GUID names
changed_functions.security_notes: A full body diff shows the sole logic change is the removal of the term (ReparseTag & 0xC0000000) != 0x40000000 from the predicate that gates entry into the tag-specific validation block. This term distinguishes tags with bit 31 clear and bit 30 set (the 0x40000000 reserved-bit pattern). IO_REPARSE_TAG_SYMLINK (0xA000000C) and IO_REPARSE_TAG_MOUNT_POINT (0xA0000003) both have bit 31 set, so the removed term never affected them; their offset/length validation is identical in both builds. A tag matching the 0x40000000 pattern reaches neither field-walk handler and falls through to return STATUS_REPARSE (0x80000005) with no memory access. Removing the term from the accepting && chain makes the predicate true for more tags, so the patched build reclassifies these reserved-bit tags from STATUS_IO_REPARSE_TAG_NOT_HANDLED (0xC0000276) to STATUS_REPARSE (0x80000005). This is a reparse-tag classification change (a relaxation), not an out-of-bounds fix.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0021F50
key_changes: Complete rewrite from request-sending function to notification processing dispatch function
Unpatched: sends SMB requests with ETW logging, reads data from buffer with offset validation
Patched: dispatches based on notification type (0-8) with different handlers for each type
Function purpose changed entirely - likely a reordering of code due to optimization differences

changed_functions.name: sub_1C006AF70
key_changes: Connection establishment function - allocates pool, registers callbacks, binds transport
Structural offsets shifted; data_1c005d* references changed
ETW/telemetry function addresses changed throughout
Added __security_cookie stack guard in patched version
changed_functions.security_notes: Connection setup function with telemetry changes. Added stack cookie in patched version is standard hardening. Not directly exploitable.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00147B0
key_changes: Connection reconnection logic - acquires spinlock, references endpoint, dispatches work
Structural refactoring with offset shifts (0x1e0->0x1c8, 0x160->0x148)
Error handling paths reorganized but same validation logic
Telemetry function references changed

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0048E24
key_changes: Buffer allocation and MDL setup function for SMB IOCTL data
Unpatched uses sub_1c001ae60 (RtlULongAdd) for overflow-checked size computation (0x34 + arg4, then + 0x50)
Patched directly computes rax = r15 + 0x34 and allocates with rax + 0x50
Removal of overflow-checking helper calls
changed_functions.security_notes: The overflow checks were replaced with direct arithmetic. The values involved (0x34 + arg4 + 0x50) are capped upstream and ExAllocatePool2 handles large sizes gracefully, so this is a simplification, not a security regression.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00204E8
key_changes: Capability negotiation flag computation with different bitmask constants
Loop iteration logic modified for capability checking
changed_functions.security_notes: Protocol capability flag constant changes - likely SMB2 dialect or version negotiation differences, not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: SmbQuicDisconnectConnection
key_changes: Patched build adds a new push-lock-guarded teardown-notification callback block
Push lock (ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx/ExReleasePushLockExclusiveEx on a1+0xA8) and the callback dereference at a1+0xB8 are introduced together as new code
New callback invokes vtable slot 3 with status 0xC000020C (STATUS_CONNECTION_DISCONNECTED)
The unpatched build has no push lock and no callback dereference at this site
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched SmbQuicDisconnectConnection (0x1C0046A74) only emits WPP traces and, under a NULL check on *(a1+0x70), calls the MsQuic shutdown vtable (MsQuic+0x88) through the CFG guard dispatcher. It contains no push lock and no callback dereference at a1+0xB8. The patched build (0x1C0046464) adds a new block that, under a newly added push lock, checks *(a1+0xB4), clears a field, and invokes v2[3](v2, 0xC000020C, v3). Because the lock and the callback are added together as new teardown code, there is no pre-existing unlocked callback for the lock to protect. This is SMB-over-QUIC teardown feature-staging, not a fix for a demonstrable use-after-free.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C001651C
key_changes: Authentication/session state processing function
Unpatched calls helper functions sub_1c0022378 and sub_1c00223b0 for status code mapping
Patched inlines the lookup logic with explicit bounds check (rax_6 > 5) before switch
ETW event IDs and data structure offsets changed
changed_functions.security_notes: The inlining of the status code lookup with bounds check is refactoring. The original helper functions already handled out-of-range values via switch defaults. No security impact.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0010520
key_changes: Connection establishment with transport binding
Struct offset shifts throughout (0x328->0x320, 0x338->0x330, 0x2e1->0x2d9, etc.)
Added sub_1c001e70c() gate check before SmbCeCheckServerEntryDialect in patched version
Telemetry function references changed
changed_functions.security_notes: Structural offset changes and added dialect check. The new gate function likely controls SMB2 vs SMB1 negotiation path. Not directly security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0016824
key_changes: Status update function with ETW logging
Unpatched calls sub_1c006927c for additional processing; patched removes this call
ETW event structure offsets changed (0x170->0x158, 0x18c->0x174)
Condition flags swapped (data_1c005da08&0x10 vs data_1c005aa08&0x10)
changed_functions.security_notes: Telemetry and status reporting changes. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00151F4
key_changes: SMB response status processing function
Checks for 0x424d53ff (SMB response) and 0x424d53fe (SMB error response) magic values
Struct offsets shifted, ETW references changed
Response processing logic reorganized but same validation checks

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0011E60
key_changes: Multi-connection dispatch function with worker thread scheduling
Struct offsets shifted (0x1b8->0x1a0, 0x1d0->0x1b8, etc.)
Connection table walk logic reorganized
ExInitializeRundownProtection offset changed (0x1d0->0x1b8)
changed_functions.security_notes: Multi-connection setup with structural refactoring. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C002009C
key_changes: Capability flag set/check function using interlocked operations
Removed unused arg4 parameter in patched version
Simplified flag comparison logic
Different bit manipulation (rcx_2 & rax_2) vs (r8_5 & 1)
changed_functions.security_notes: Capability flag manipulation refactoring. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0010D80
key_changes: Global data reference changes (data_1c006804b -> data_1c006504b)
Telemetry and timing function references changed

changed_functions.name: SmbCryptoAllocateAndPopulateCipherSuiteOrder
key_changes: Simplified error handling - returns pointer directly instead of through output parameter
Same allocation size formula (arg2 * 0x1a - 2 + 4) in both versions
Removed unused output parameter arg3
changed_functions.security_notes: Refactoring of cipher suite allocation. Same allocation logic, cleaner error path.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000E09C
key_changes: Object cleanup/destructor function
Reordered ExWaitForRundownProtectionRelease before resource cleanup
Changed memset size (0x78 -> 0x60) for structure zeroing
Function pointer offsets shifted in vtable access
changed_functions.security_notes: Object cleanup with reordered operations. The reordering places ExWaitForRundownProtectionRelease first in the patched version, which is safer teardown ordering but not a direct security fix.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C007E078
key_changes: Feature configuration initialization completely rewritten
Patched version adds __security_cookie and iterates arrays differently
Feature flag query logic changed with RtlQueryFeatureConfiguration / WIL feature-staging
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature configuration / WIL feature-staging initialization. Servicing churn, not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: FsRtlValidateChangeNotifyBuffer
key_changes: Standalone FsRtlValidateChangeNotifyBufferEx and FsRtlValidateRelativeFileName helpers inlined into a single validator in the patched build
Inlined patched form performs no more validation than the unpatched wrapper + Ex helper pair
changed_functions.security_notes: Shared OS FsRtl library refactor (inlining). The patched inlined validator does not add validation over the unpatched pair; if anything it validates less (the filename-content validation via FsRtlValidateRelativeFileName is folded away). Not an mrxsmb attacker-data tightening.

changed_functions.name: SmbCompressionDecompress
key_changes: Unpatched checks the server-claimed size (*a2 > a5) before calling RtlDecompressBufferEx2 with *a2 as the uncompressed size
Patched removes that check and instead passes the true output buffer size a5 to RtlDecompressBufferEx2
changed_functions.security_notes: Equivalent-safety refactor. Both builds bound the decompress write to <= a5 (unpatched via the *a2 <= a5 precheck, patched by passing a5 directly as UncompressedBufferSize), and both retain the post-check on the produced size. No out-of-bounds write is introduced or removed; if anything the patched form drops a redundant precheck. Not a security tightening.

security_findings.entry_point: REPARSE_DATA_BUFFER validation (reparse data received during path/reparse handling)
security_findings.description: Previously suspected as an out-of-bounds validation bypass. Verification against both builds shows the only logic change in FsRtlValidateReparsePointBufferEx is the removal of a single tag-acceptance predicate term, (ReparseTag & 0xC0000000) != 0x40000000. The symbolic-link (0xA000000C) and mount-point (0xA0000003) offset/length validation (the RtlULongAdd accumulation and bounds comparisons) is byte-identical in both builds. The removed term only distinguishes tags with bit 31 clear and bit 30 set (0x40000000 reserved-bit pattern), which are never symlink or mount-point tags. Such a tag reaches no field-walk handler and returns STATUS_REPARSE (0x80000005) with no memory access. Removing the term makes the predicate true for more tags, reclassifying these reserved-bit tags from STATUS_IO_REPARSE_TAG_NOT_HANDLED (0xC0000276) to STATUS_REPARSE (0x80000005). This is a reparse-tag classification change (a relaxation), with no reachable out-of-bounds read or write in either build.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: None. The changed predicate only reclassifies certain reserved-bit reparse tags; it does not alter the symlink/mount-point field validation that server-supplied reparse data would exercise, and the reclassified tags reach no offset/length arithmetic or memory access.
security_findings.primitive: None. No reachable out-of-bounds read/write in either build.
security_findings.assembly:
// UNPATCHED mrxsmb_unpatched.sys FsRtlValidateReparsePointBufferEx @ 0x1C0034840
0x1C0034919 : and eax, 0C0000000h    // ReparseTag & 0xC0000000
0x1C003491E : cmp eax, 40000000h     // == 0x40000000 ? (removed in patched)
0x1C0034987 : cmp ecx, 0A000000Ch    // IO_REPARSE_TAG_SYMLINK (identical in both)
0x1C0034CB5 : cmp ecx, 0A0000003h    // IO_REPARSE_TAG_MOUNT_POINT (identical in both)
// PATCHED mrxsmb_patched.sys FsRtlValidateReparsePointBufferEx @ 0x1C0032620
0x1C0032755 : cmp ecx, 0A000000Ch
0x1C0032A83 : cmp ecx, 0A0000003h

breakpoints.why: Anchor for confirming the (non-security) predicate change. The unpatched build executes 'and eax,0C0000000h; cmp eax,40000000h' at 0x1C0034919/0x1C003491E; the patched build at 0x1C0032620 has no such pair.

key_offsets: 0x1C0034919: and eax, 0C0000000h - removed tag-acceptance term (unpatched only)
0x1C003491E: cmp eax, 40000000h - removed tag-acceptance term (unpatched only)
0x1C0034987 / 0x1C0032755: cmp ecx, 0A000000Ch - IO_REPARSE_TAG_SYMLINK (both builds, identical validation)
debugging_aids.expected_observation: No memory-safety fault is reachable through this change in either build.
debugging_aids.notes: The symlink/mount-point offset/length validation is identical across builds; only the tag-acceptance predicate differs by one term.

security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (SMB-over-QUIC teardown feature code added)
security_findings.description: Previously suspected as a missing-synchronization / use-after-free fix. Verification against both builds shows the unpatched SmbQuicDisconnectConnection (0x1C0046A74) contains no push lock and no callback dereference at a1+0xB8; it only emits WPP traces and calls the MsQuic shutdown vtable (MsQuic+0x88) under a NULL check on *(a1+0x70). The patched build (0x1C0046464) ADDS a new block that, under a newly added push lock (ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx/ExReleasePushLockExclusiveEx on a1+0xA8), checks *(a1+0xB4), clears a field, and invokes vtable slot 3 with status 0xC000020C (STATUS_CONNECTION_DISCONNECTED). Because the lock and the callback are introduced together as new teardown code, there is no pre-existing unlocked callback in the unpatched build for the lock to protect. This is SMB-over-QUIC teardown feature-staging, not a fix for a demonstrable race/UAF.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: None. The unpatched build has no unlocked callback invocation at this site, so there is no race window to trigger.
security_findings.primitive: None. No demonstrable use-after-free in the unpatched build.

breakpoints.why: Anchor for confirming the added (patched-only) push-lock-guarded teardown callback. The push-lock calls at 0x1C0046508 and 0x1C0046549 and the callback status at 0x1C0046524 do not exist in the unpatched function at 0x1C0046A74.

debugging_aids.expected_observation: The unpatched function contains no unlocked callback invocation, so no use-after-free is reachable through this site.
debugging_aids.notes: The push lock and the callback are added together as new SMB-over-QUIC teardown code; they do not retrofit synchronization onto pre-existing vulnerable code.

summary: No delivered security fix was found in this diff. The two changes initially suspected as security-relevant do not survive verification. In FsRtlValidateReparsePointBufferEx the only logic change is removal of a single tag-acceptance predicate term ((ReparseTag & 0xC0000000) != 0x40000000); the symbolic-link and mount-point offset/length validation is byte-identical in both builds, and the removed term only affects reserved-bit reparse tags that reach no field walk, so no out-of-bounds primitive exists (the change is a classification relaxation). In SmbQuicDisconnectConnection the patched build adds a new push-lock-guarded teardown-notification callback; the unpatched build has no unlocked callback at that site, so the lock and callback are new SMB-over-QUIC teardown code (feature-staging), not a use-after-free fix. The remainder of the diff is servicing/feature churn: SMB-over-QUIC feature code (including removal of the QUIC certificate-mapping and server-suffix feature), WIL feature-staging helpers, WPP/ETW tracing changes, and struct-offset relocations. An independent review of the security-relevant-looking changed functions (validation, server-response parsing, allocation-size and copy-length computation, receive/decompress/decrypt paths) found no function where the patched build adds or tightens a bounds/length/overflow/type/lock check on attacker-controlled data.
